# Extra slides: var r2_label

## The YAIB Experimental Pipeline {auto-animate="true"}



![](figures/yaib/yaib_flow_combined.svg){fig-cap="YAIB Experimental Pipeline" width=120%}
  <div class="fragment fade-out" data-fragment-index="1"
       style="position:absolute; top:10%; left:47.5%; width:50%; height:100%; background:white; z-index:2;"></div>

::: {.columns}
::: {.center .column width="50%" }
<div style="text-align: center; margin-top: 0.4em;">*Creation of harmonized cohorts*</div>
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
<div style="text-align: center; margin-top: 0.4em;">*Benchmarking Framework*</div>
:::
:::
<div data-id="recipies-logo-box" style="position:absolute; right:38%; top:30%; width:5%; text-align:left;">
  <img src="figures/yaib/recipies_logo.svg" style="width:100%;" class="no-dark">
</div>
::: {.notes}
- Ideally, clinical experts inject their clinical domain knowledge of the respective dataset into the dataset and task definitions
- ML researchers can then focus on the modeling and evaluation, while being able to easily reproduce results across datasets and preprocessing choices.
:::

## ReciPies: A Simple Modular Preprocessing Framework for ML Research {auto-animate="true"}
![](figures/yaib/recipies_flow.svg){fig-cap="ReciPies Flow" width=100% .no-dark}

<div data-id="recipies-logo-box" style="position:absolute; right:2%; top:80%; width:12%; text-align:right;">
  <img src="figures/yaib/recipies_logo.svg" style="width:100%;" class="no-dark">
</div>

::: {.notes}
- ReciPies is a modular preprocessing framework that allows researchers to define and execute data transformation
- UML type diagrams of the main components and their interactions.
::: 

## Datasets and Tasks

Four publicly available ICU datasets:

<!-- - MIMIC-IV^[[@johnsonMIMICIVFreelyAccessible2023]]
- eICU^[[@pollardEICUCollaborativeResearch2018]]
- HiRID^[[@hylandEarlyPredictionCirculatory2020]]
- AUMCdb^[[@thoralSharingICUPatient2021]] -->
| **Dataset**                  | **MIMIC-III / IV** | **eICU** | **HiRID** | **AUMCdb** |
|------------------------------|--------------------|----------|-----------|------------|
| **Stays**                  | 40k (0.1k) / 73k | 201k (2k) | 34k       | 23k        |
| **Version**                 | v1.4 / v2.2        | v2.0     | v1.1.1    | v1.0.2     |
| **Frequency (time-series)** | 1 hour             | 5 minutes| 2 / 5 minutes | up to 1 minute |
| **Origin**                  | USA                | USA      | Switzerland | Netherlands |
| **Originally published**    | 2015^[[@johnsonMIMICIIIFreelyAccessible2016]] / 2020^[[@johnsonMIMICIVFreelyAccessible2023]] | 2017^[[@pollardEICUCollaborativeResearch2018]] | 2020^[[@hylandEarlyPredictionCirculatory2020]] | 2019^[[@thoralSharingICUPatient2021]] |

**Five common ICU prediction tasks**: Mortality, Acute Kidney Injury, Sepsis, Kidney Function, Length of Stay.

## Prediction Task Overview {data-scale="0.1"}

::: {.notes}
**Note:** The related work is non-exhaustive.
:::

::: {.small-text}
| **No** | **Task**         | **Frequency**       | **Type** | **Related Work**                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|--------|------------------|---------------------|----------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1      | Mortality        | Once per stay*     | C        | [@bakerContinuousAutomaticMortality2020; @luMachineLearningBased2022a; @medicEvidencebasedClinicalDecision2019; @sharmaMortalityPredictionICU2017; @syedApplicationMachineLearning2021a]                        |
| 2      | Acute Kidney Injury (AKI) | Hourly             | C        |   [@huangInterpretableTemporalConvolutional2021; @nikkinenDevelopingSupervisedMachine2022; @panSelfCorrectingDeepLearning2019; @rankDeeplearningbasedRealtimePrediction2020; @shamoutMachineLearningClinical2021; @wangRealTimePredictionAKI2020; @koynerDevelopmentMachineLearning2018] |
| 3      | Sepsis           | Hourly             | C        | [@kokAutomatedPredictionSepsis2020; @lauritsenEarlyDetectionSepsis2020; @merathUseMachineLearning2020; @fleurenMachineLearningIntensive2020; @moorPredictingSepsisMultisite2021; @moorEarlyRecognitionSepsis2019a; @muralitharanMachineLearningBased2021; @reynaEarlyPredictionSepsis2019; @shamoutMachineLearningClinical2021; @wangIntegratingPhysiologicalTime2022] |
| 4      | Kidney Function (KF)               | Once per stay*     | R        | [@tomasevClinicallyApplicableApproach2019; @futomaPredictingDiseaseProgression2016; @perotteRiskPredictionChronic2015; @chengPredictingInpatientAcute2018]                                                    |
| 5      | Length of Stay (LoS)              | Hourly             | R        | [@shillanUseMachineLearning2019; @guoEvaluationTimeSeries2020]                                                                                                                                             |
:::
- **C**: Classification task  
- **R**: Regression task  
- *Once per stay*: Indicates predictions are made once during the ICU stay.

---

## Transfer Learning

- What if we train a generalized model on "most generalizable" eICU?
- Finetune on "difficult" HiRID

::: {.fragment}

::: {.column width="50%"}
AUROC:
![](figures/yaib/transfer_learning.svg){fig-cap="" width=100% .no-dark}

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
AUPRC:
![](figures/yaib/transfer_learning_auprc.svg){fig-cap="" width=100% .no-dark}

:::
:::

## External Validation with Mortality

::: {.column width="50%"}
AUROC:
![](figures/yaib/ext_val_mortality_pooled_auroc.svg){fig-cap="ReciPies Flow" width=100% .no-dark}

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
AUPRC:
![](figures/yaib/ext_val_mortality_pooled_auprc.svg){fig-cap="ReciPies Flow" width=100% .no-dark}

:::

::: {.fragment}

- **Column**: Model trained on dataset
- **Row**: Model evaluated on dataset
- **Diagonal**: Internal validation (same dataset)

:::
